name: marketing / editorial
activates: web-animation-design when motion is part of the concept
skips: dense utilitarian defaults as the main aesthetic unless the brief mixes modes
designPosture: rhythm and pacing matter as much as correctness
responsive behavior should preserve the story, not just the layout
qualityBar: the page has a point of view
sections have varied rhythm and visual weight
motion, if present, feels purposeful and interruptible
mobile gets its own thoughtful translation when needed
the work feels designed, not merely assembled
recommendedInputs: asset direction: imagery, illustration, motion, typography
DESIGN.md or project identity notes with atmosphere language
humanJudgmentRequired: whether the concept is actually distinctive or just louder
when motion improves the story vs distracts from it
how far to push personality before usability suffers
what should change completely on mobile to preserve impact
promptFraming: build a page with a strong editorial point of view
treat spacing and rhythm as part of the message
make the mobile version preserve the emotional arc, not just the blocks
avoid generic saas gradients and stock launch-page tropes

skillsFired: whimsical-design, world-build, or web-animation-design only when the brief earns them
skillsSkipped: dense utilitarian defaults unless the brief mixes modes
humanJudgment: whether the concept has a real point of view
how far expression should be pushed
